evidence.question_text_raw: - What grade are you in? What are you studying? What's your major? Why did you choose this major? Is daily learning pressure high? How do you typically spend a day when you have classes? How do you arrange your time when you don't have classes?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
I usually wake up at 8:00 AM. If there are any household responsibilities, I take care of them before starting my workday. After that, I leave for the market and begin my daily business activities.
My work also involves managing payments and coordinating orders. I usually reach the market around 10:00 AM. If there is a large order or a shipment arriving from a major company, I need to oversee and manage the entire process, which often keeps me occupied throughout the day. I continue working until around 5:00 PM.
From 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM, I focus on closing tasks, completing paperwork, and finalizing daily transactions. After finishing work, I return home and spend quality time with my family.

What do you mostly use your time on outside of work or studies?

I do not use Snapchat.

evidence.answer_text_raw:
When I visited the mobile shop, I was initially looking to purchase a second-hand smartphone. However, the shopkeeper showed me a brand-new Infinix device and explained its features. After learning about its specifications and considering its budget-friendly price, I decided to purchase the Infinix phone instead.
In my opinion, Chinese smartphone brands should maintain consistent quality standards and establish clear pricing benchmarks. If customers see reliable quality across all devices, the perception that these brands offer low-quality products will gradually disappear.
Generally, low-budget smartphones from Chinese brands are often perceived as lower quality because the brands may not be as well-known. On the other hand, if a well-established brand like Samsung launches a budget smartphone, consumers are less likely to associate it with poor quality because of the brand's strong reputation and trust in the market.

evidence.question_text_raw:
Can you please quickly walk me through how you use your smartphone from morning till night? (Moderator: Try to get and establish top 3 specific usage categories based on user's phone usage)
1. Communication & Social Connection
How frequently do you connect with your friends and family using your smartphone?
How do you typically communicate with friends, family, or colleagues using your smartphone?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Do you prefer voice calls, messaging, or video chats?
Any challenges that you face during communication? Probe in detail
2. Entertainment
What do you usually do for entertainment on your smartphone? e.g. watch videos, scroll social media, play games, or listen to music?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Any kind of difficulties that you face while using entertaining apps?
 
3. Work & Study
Are you using your smartphone for any work- or study-related tasks?
What kind of apps or tools do you use?
How does it help you with productivity or learning?
Are there any challenges you face when using a smartphone for work or education?
4. Shopping, Online Services & Transactions
Using your phone for online shopping, mobile banking, or paying bills?
What was the experience like? 
In what situations do you prefer using your phone over going physically to a bank/shop?
5. Camera Usage (Photos, Videos & Creativity)
How often do you take photos or make videos with your phone? 
What kind of edits you apply or share photos/videos? If so, how and with which apps?
Moderator to confirm & assure: Are there any other major usages? 
Overall how satisfied are you with your current phone? If the full score for satisfaction is 10 points, what is your current satisfaction score? Why is it this score, and specifically, what are the aspects you are satisfied and dissatisfied with?
evidence.answer_text_raw:
Communication
I primarily use my mobile phone for business-related communication through WhatsApp. For calls, I use my SIM card regularly. Most of my communication consists of messages, voice notes, sharing pictures, and sending invoices to customers and business contacts. Therefore, WhatsApp is one of the most important applications on my phone.
Entertainment
For entertainment and information, I mainly use Instagram and Facebook. I am particularly interested in current affairs and like to stay updated on what is happening in Pakistan. I also frequently watch content related to the Pakistan Army and national affairs.
Online Banking & Transactions
I use Faysal Bank and Easypaisa applications on my mobile phone for financial transactions, payments, and banking services.
Camera & Device Expectations
I am generally satisfied with the camera performance of my current mobile phone. However, I believe future models should offer further improvements in camera quality.
I also feel that the battery capacity is relatively low at 5,200mAh. For my next smartphone, I would prefer a larger battery, ideally between 6,500mAh and 7,000mAh, to provide longer usage time.
In my opinion, Infinix should continue improving the features of its upcoming smartphone series. The company should focus on enhancing camera quality beyond the current 50MP setup. For example, some competing smartphones in the market offer 108MP cameras. Therefore, I believe Infinix devices in the PKR 60,000 price segment should provide at least an 80MP camera or equivalent image quality to remain competitive.

evidence.answer_text_raw:
I started working in 2012, and for the past eight years, I have been running my own business.
Whenever I need to purchase a product, I first check its reviews and evaluate the brand before making a decision. I do not usually buy products based solely on advertisements.
Since I work in the market and have experience dealing with different suppliers, I prefer to compare prices and quality before making a purchase. For example, when buying a product, I often visit 8 to 10 different shops to compare rates and quality, and only then do I make my final decision.
Currently, I am planning to purchase a laptop. I intend to visit Hafeez Center and compare prices, specifications, and available options across several shops before selecting the most suitable one.
I do not rely on advertisements for purchase decisions. Instead, my decision-making process is primarily based on market research, product comparisons, and face-to-face interactions with sellers.
According to my personality, three words best describe me: 
need-based
Cost-effective
Reliable.
I make decisions based on actual needs rather than impulse purchases. Before buying anything, I carefully evaluate its value, quality, and usefulness. I always look for cost-effective options that provide the best balance between price and performance.

Since I work in the market and have experience dealing with different suppliers, I prefer to compare prices and quality before making a purchase. For example, when buying a product, I often visit 8 to 10 different shops to compare rates and quality, and only then do I make my final decision.
Currently, I am planning to purchase a laptop. I intend to visit Hafeez Center and compare prices, specifications, and available options across several shops before selecting the most suitable one.
I do not rely on advertisements for purchase decisions. Instead, my decision-making process is primarily based on market research, product comparisons, and face-to-face interactions with sellers.
According to my personality, three words best describe me: 
need-based
Cost-effective
Reliable.
I make decisions based on actual needs rather than impulse purchases. Before buying anything, I carefully evaluate its value, quality, and usefulness. I always look for cost-effective options that provide the best balance between price and performance.

How many phones do you own currently? Please tell me the names & models
if more than 1 than what are the reasons and purposes of using more than 1 phone?

evidence.answer_text_raw:
I am currently using the Infinix Hot 60 Pro. The phone's retail price was PKR 54,000, but I purchased it for PKR 48,000 after receiving a discount. I bought the device approximately five to six months ago.
Before this, I was using the Samsung Galaxy A51, which I purchased for around PKR 52,000. However, I frequently experienced heating issues with that device.
Compared to my previous phone, the Infinix Hot 60 Pro performs much better. It is very smooth, does not lag, and I have not experienced any heating issues so far. Overall, it has been a very good device for my daily use.
One of my friends recommended the Infinix Hot 60 Pro. After checking its reviews and specifications, I found it to be a suitable option. The phone offers 8GB RAM and 128GB storage, has excellent battery life, and its processor performs noticeably better than the Samsung Galaxy A51.
My Samsung device often lagged, and there were times when WhatsApp messages would appear 5–10 minutes late. Due to these issues, I decided to switch to a new phone and chose the Infinix Hot 60 Pro.
I purchased the device brand new in a box-packed condition.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
How would you describe your spending philosophy? 
(Spontaneous vs. Planned, Advance Consumption/loans vs. Living within One's Means, Buy What You Like vs. Careful Selection) Can you give an example? 
How do you usually spend your personal income or pocket money in a typical month?
Probes: What are your main spending categories? (e.g., food, outings, gadgets, gaming, clothing, etc.)
How important is spending on gadgets or your phone compared to other things? Do you plan your spending or is it more spontaneous? 
How do you usually spend money/pocket money?
Do you generally try to buy and spend on new or innovative products first in the market or do you wait and first learn from others experiences or wait for discounts?
1) If you were to describe your personality when it comes to spending money on things in three words, how would you describe it?
2) When it comes to buying mobile phone, which type do you think you aare more like: such as 1) Peopler who are willing to spend a little mmore for what they like 2) People who are willing to spend cost-effectiveness in everything 3)People who pursue novelty and enjoy trying new things 4) People who follow those around them/word of mouth
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
I started working in 2012, and for the past eight years, I have been running my own business.
Whenever I need to purchase a product, I first check its reviews and evaluate the brand before making a decision. I do not usually buy products based solely on advertisements.
Since I work in the market and have experience dealing with different suppliers, I prefer to compare prices and quality before making a purchase. For example, when buying a product, I often visit 8 to 10 different shops to compare rates and quality, and only then do I make my final decision.
Currently, I am planning to purchase a laptop. I intend to visit Hafeez Center and compare prices, specifications, and available options across several shops before selecting the most suitable one.
I do not rely on advertisements for purchase decisions. Instead, my decision-making process is primarily based on market research, product comparisons, and face-to-face interactions with sellers.
According to my personality, three words best describe me: 
need-based
Cost-effective
Reliable.
I make decisions based on actual needs rather than impulse purchases. Before buying anything, I carefully evaluate its value, quality, and usefulness. I always look for cost-effective options that provide the best balance between price and performance.

What are the additional benefits attract you when you buy a product e.g. discounts, gifts, memberships, collaboration with other brands/ products etc.
What type of discounts, gifts or memberships do you expect that will excite you and why?

all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
Without a mobile phone, communication and daily life would become much more difficult. Mobile phones have become an essential part of modern life, helping people stay connected with family, friends, customers, and business contacts.
For me, a mobile phone is especially important because most of my business communication and order management take place through it. Without a mobile device, managing work, staying informed, and maintaining communication would be significantly more challenging.
Having discounts on mobile purchases would be very attractive and valuable for me.
I would highly appreciate receiving useful accessories such as earbuds or a high-quality handsfree as a complimentary gift with the mobile phone.
However, I would not be interested in promotional gifts such as power banks, selfie sticks, or similar accessories, as they are not particularly useful to me.

How many phones do you own currently? Please tell me the names & models
if more than 1 than what are the reasons and purposes of using more than 1 phone?
What is your current main/ primary phone?
What was the brand and model of your previous main mobile phone?
Why did you replace your previous phone?
When do you normally replace or buy a new phone for yourself? (purchase frequency)
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
I am currently using the Infinix Hot 60 Pro. The phone's retail price was PKR 54,000, but I purchased it for PKR 48,000 after receiving a discount. I bought the device approximately five to six months ago.
Before this, I was using the Samsung Galaxy A51, which I purchased for around PKR 52,000. However, I frequently experienced heating issues with that device.
Compared to my previous phone, the Infinix Hot 60 Pro performs much better. It is very smooth, does not lag, and I have not experienced any heating issues so far. Overall, it has been a very good device for my daily use.
One of my friends recommended the Infinix Hot 60 Pro. After checking its reviews and specifications, I found it to be a suitable option. The phone offers 8GB RAM and 128GB storage, has excellent battery life, and its processor performs noticeably better than the Samsung Galaxy A51.
My Samsung device often lagged, and there were times when WhatsApp messages would appear 5–10 minutes late. Due to these issues, I decided to switch to a new phone and chose the Infinix Hot 60 Pro.
I purchased the device brand new in a box-packed condition.

all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
When I visited the mobile shop, I was initially looking to purchase a second-hand smartphone. However, the shopkeeper showed me a brand-new Infinix device and explained its features. After learning about its specifications and considering its budget-friendly price, I decided to purchase the Infinix phone instead.
In my opinion, Chinese smartphone brands should maintain consistent quality standards and establish clear pricing benchmarks. If customers see reliable quality across all devices, the perception that these brands offer low-quality products will gradually disappear.
Generally, low-budget smartphones from Chinese brands are often perceived as lower quality because the brands may not be as well-known. On the other hand, if a well-established brand like Samsung launches a budget smartphone, consumers are less likely to associate it with poor quality because of the brand's strong reputation and trust in the market.

all_evidence.question_text_raw:
Can you please quickly walk me through how you use your smartphone from morning till night? (Moderator: Try to get and establish top 3 specific usage categories based on user's phone usage)
1. Communication & Social Connection
How frequently do you connect with your friends and family using your smartphone?
How do you typically communicate with friends, family, or colleagues using your smartphone?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Do you prefer voice calls, messaging, or video chats?
Any challenges that you face during communication? Probe in detail
2. Entertainment
What do you usually do for entertainment on your smartphone? e.g. watch videos, scroll social media, play games, or listen to music?
Which apps do you use and why those?
Any kind of difficulties that you face while using entertaining apps?
 
3. Work & Study
Are you using your smartphone for any work- or study-related tasks?
What kind of apps or tools do you use?
How does it help you with productivity or learning?
Are there any challenges you face when using a smartphone for work or education?
4. Shopping, Online Services & Transactions
Using your phone for online shopping, mobile banking, or paying bills?
What was the experience like? 
In what situations do you prefer using your phone over going physically to a bank/shop?
5. Camera Usage (Photos, Videos & Creativity)
How often do you take photos or make videos with your phone? 
What kind of edits you apply or share photos/videos? If so, how and with which apps?
Moderator to confirm & assure: Are there any other major usages? 
Overall how satisfied are you with your current phone? If the full score for satisfaction is 10 points, what is your current satisfaction score? Why is it this score, and specifically, what are the aspects you are satisfied and dissatisfied with?
all_evidence.answer_text_raw:
Communication
I primarily use my mobile phone for business-related communication through WhatsApp. For calls, I use my SIM card regularly. Most of my communication consists of messages, voice notes, sharing pictures, and sending invoices to customers and business contacts. Therefore, WhatsApp is one of the most important applications on my phone.
Entertainment
For entertainment and information, I mainly use Instagram and Facebook. I am particularly interested in current affairs and like to stay updated on what is happening in Pakistan. I also frequently watch content related to the Pakistan Army and national affairs.
Online Banking & Transactions
I use Faysal Bank and Easypaisa applications on my mobile phone for financial transactions, payments, and banking services.
Camera & Device Expectations
I am generally satisfied with the camera performance of my current mobile phone. However, I believe future models should offer further improvements in camera quality.
I also feel that the battery capacity is relatively low at 5,200mAh. For my next smartphone, I would prefer a larger battery, ideally between 6,500mAh and 7,000mAh, to provide longer usage time.
In my opinion, Infinix should continue improving the features of its upcoming smartphone series. The company should focus on enhancing camera quality beyond the current 50MP setup. For example, some competing smartphones in the market offer 108MP cameras. Therefore, I believe Infinix devices in the PKR 60,000 price segment should provide at least an 80MP camera or equivalent image quality to remain competitive.

rows.question_text_raw: - Do you have any plan for future occupation? Do you have any role models? What do you expect yourself to be in the next 5 years?

- Let's talk about the phones you have owned. Tell me about your last three smartphones — starting from the one before your current phone, going back.
▸ Device 1 (most recent before current): Brand & model? Approximate price? Year purchased?
▸ Device 2: Brand & model? Approximate price? Year?
▸ Device 3 (oldest): Brand & model? Approximate price? Year?
- Looking at these three phones — how often have you been changing your phone, roughly?▸ Every 1 year? 2 years? 3+ years?▸ Was the replacement planned or forced (broken, stolen, lost)?

- We just know about your privious devices. Generally under what circumstances do you usually choose to replace your phone with a new one? 
- What were the reasons that drove you to change phones for each of your previous phones?
- When did you start considering buying a new phone (before you purchased the current one)? Why did you start considering at that time?

- Take me back to the time when you first started thinking about changing your previous phone. What happened?
- How long before buying did you start thinking about it?
- [Pain Point ] Is there pain points with the previous phone? [IF YES]
- What were the main problems or frustrations with your previous device that pushed you to change?
▸ Battery? Performance? Camera? Storage? Software?
▸ Display issues? Heating? Hanging or crashing?
▸ Were these issues there from the start, or did they develop over time?

Before you started looking, what were the specific things you wanted in your new phone?
▸ Must-have features — what were the top 3?
▸ Any features you had heard about and wanted to try?
▸ Were there any deal-breakers — things you absolutely did not want?
- What are the reasons for considering such specific requirements? How did you evaluate and measure your needs and the functions that the product can provide?

- [Circle Influence] Who influenced you to be interested in it? Through what channels did you learn about it? What aspect attracted you? Do you still remember the specific content that influenced you at that time?

- Did you have a budget before purchase? What was the budget range? How was this budget formed? What were the upper and lower limits of the budget respectively? Did the budget increase or decrease compared with previous phone? Why?
- [For users who upgraded budget] What are the reason for the upgrading and how did you consider the significant increase in budget compared to the previous one?
- [For users who downgraded budget] What are the reason for the downgrading and how did you consider the significant decrease in budget compared to the previous one?

- [For brands the candidate are aware of] How did you come into contact with these brands？What information can help you make a comparison? After receiving this message, why would you approve? [Note: verify whether candidate only considers brands that has been used and he/she is familiar with (small-scale screening), or considers 1-2 brands within the target range (medium-scale), or considers a wide range (including all mainstream brands in the market)]

From where would you like to get details of this phone? 
What should this phone do to reach out to you?
What benefits you are expecting out of this phone?
Ovelall Feedback on phone
- Where do you usually prefer to learn about relevant information on mobile phones before purchasing one?

- Why do you choose these channels to learn about mobile phone information? What are the differences between different channels? [Note: Dig deep into how channel awareness is formed] If the information from different channels varies, which channel's information feedback will you prioritize?

- Among your colleagues, friends, and family members around you, what brands of mobile phone do they use? Is there someone you trust his opinion about mobile phones? Why do you trust his opinion? How did they recommend it to you? Why can this point impress you?

- What is the order of your information search? How do you use different channels (such as Google, social media, etc.)? [Note: Explore the formation of differentiated positioning and usage of channels, and the reasons behind. For example, some channel is used for avoiding pitfalls screening and some is for price comparison]

- [Self-initiated online search] Which platform is mainly used for searching? Why choose this platform? What keywords will be searched? What are the impressions of Google, YT...? Which platform is most trusted, and why?

- [Self-experience offline] Which aspect attracted you during the introduction at offline stores or by sales promoters? [For students] What offline activities are you interested in? What activities on campus can students actively participate in?

- [Passive Information Reach] Offline big brand advertisements, online official websites, press conferences (watch LIVE/ read text and image summaries), video websites (Ins, YT, etc.), e-commerce websites (advertising recommendations/user reviews), tech bloggers, Google, professional website reviews, variety shows, shopping guide Moments, etc.
- After learning about this/these phone(s), have you continued to use any channels to further understand these phones? (For example, through what keywords and on which platforms did you search?)
- After the search is completed, what information (brand/model/unique selling point/review) caught your attention? What specifically do you remember? Why were you attracted?
